name: fastText (Facebook Word Vectors & Text Classification)

auth.notes: Local library and CLI; no auth

performance.notes: C++ backend; extremely fast training and inference; millions of docs/sec

agent_readiness.notes: Facebook's word vector and text classification library. import fasttext. model = fasttext.train_supervised('data.train.txt', epoch=25, lr=1.0, wordNgrams=2). model.predict('query text', k=3). Unsupervised: fasttext.train_unsupervised('corpus.txt', model='skipgram'). Pre-trained vectors for 157 languages (cc.en.300.bin). Language identification model (lid.176.bin). Subword embeddings handle OOV words. Extremely fast C++ backend. Tab-separated label format: __label__category text.

gotchas: Less accurate than transformers for complex NLP tasks
best_when: Fast text classification at massive scale (millions of docs)
Word embeddings for low-resource languages (157 language vectors)
When transformer inference cost is prohibitive
avoid_when: Complex NLP tasks needing contextual understanding (use transformers)
When GPU acceleration is key (fasttext CPU-only)
summary: fastText provides fast word embeddings and text classification via C++ backend. train_supervised('data.txt').predict('text') for classification. Pre-trained vectors for 157 languages. Language ID model (176 languages). Subword embeddings handle OOV. Millions of documents/second. Best when transformer cost is prohibitive or for language ID. Use __label__class format for supervised training.
